---
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| include: false
source("setup.R")
```


```{r}
#| fig-show: hold
#| fig-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| out-width: "100%"

# Astrology
astro_plot <- stupiddf3 %>%
  filter(AstrologicalSign !="No Response") %>%
  dplyr::count(AstrologicalSign) %>%
  mutate(
    pct = n / sum(n),
    label = AstrologicalSign
  )
symbol_map <- c(
  "Aries" = "♈ ",
  "Taurus" = "♉ ",
  "Gemini" = "♊ ",
  "Cancer" = "♋ ",
  "Leo" = "♌ ",
  "Virgo" = "♍ ",
  "Libra" = "♎ ",
  "Scorpio" = "♏ ",
  "Sagittarius" = "♐ ",
  "Capricorn" = "♑ ",
  "Aquarius" = "♒ ",
  "Pisces" = "♓ "
  
)
astro_plot$AstrologicalSign <- symbol_map[astro_plot$AstrologicalSign]

# Build the astrology plot
astro_plot_fig <- ggplot(astro_plot, aes(x = 2, y = pct, fill = AstrologicalSign)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = AstrologicalSign),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3.8,
    color = "white",
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "#2563EB", "#14B8A6", "#F59E0B", "#EC4899",
    "#8B5CF6", "#06B6D4", "#84CC16", "#F97316",
    "#6366F1", "#EF4444", "#0EA5E9", "#A855F7"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Astrological Signs",
    subtitle = "32% of respondents did not report an astrological sign"
  ) +
  theme_void(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# IQDF
iqdf <- stupiddf3 %>%
  dplyr::count(TestedIQ)
iqdf$TestedIQ[is.na(iqdf$TestedIQ)] <- "No Response"
#clean + order factor
iqdf <- iqdf %>%
  mutate(
    percent = n / sum(n) * 100,
    
    TestedIQ = factor(
      TestedIQ,
      levels = c(
        "No Response",
        "80-99",
        "100-119",
        "120-139",
        "140-159",
        "160-179",
        "over 180"
      )
    )
  )

# Build the IQ plot
iq_plot_fig <- ggplot(iqdf, aes(x = TestedIQ, y = percent, fill = percent)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(percent, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.15
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#E9D5FF",
    high = "#8B5CF6"
  ) +
  theme_site()

# Print both plots
astro_plot_fig
iq_plot_fig
```
